name: 2D Airfoil (Tutorial)
description: Stock OpenFOAM tutorial: 2D NACA airfoil at 8° angle of attack. Pre-meshed C-grid (~10k cells), Spalart-Allmaras turbulence model. Run this first to verify your setup is working.

mesh.title: Pre-Meshed Case
mesh.description: This case comes with a pre-generated C-grid mesh. No meshing commands needed — the mesh is already in constant/polyMesh.
mesh.tip: The mesh uses ~10,000 cells arranged in a C-topology around the airfoil. You can inspect it in system/blockMeshDict.

boundaries.title: Boundary Conditions
boundaries.description: Inlet velocity at 26 m/s (8° angle of attack), zero-gradient outlet, slip walls.
boundaries.tip: The inlet velocity is decomposed into (25.75, 3.62, 0) m/s to achieve 8° AoA.

solver.title: Solver Settings
solver.description: SIMPLE algorithm with Spalart-Allmaras turbulence model. 500 iterations.
solver.tip: Watch for residuals dropping below 1e-4 — that indicates convergence.

run.title: Run Simulation
run.description: Execute simpleFoam. This pre-meshed case typically converges in 1-3 minutes.
run.tip: If this completes successfully, your Docker and OpenFOAM setup is working correctly.

postprocess.title: View Results
postprocess.description: Visualize pressure coefficient (Cp) distribution and velocity field around the airfoil.
postprocess.tip: In ParaView, use 'Plot Over Line' along the airfoil surface to see the Cp distribution.
